channel.title: Entertainment News | CableTV.com
channel.description: Super fans, rejoice! Our entertainment news keeps you passionate about sports, TV, and streaming.

item.title: US Judge Pauses Paramount-Warner Bros. Merger — For Now
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: A federal judge in California granted a temporary restraining order on the Paramount-Warner Bros. merger. The deal is on pause for at least 14 days.
item.content:encoded:
Paramount-WBD merger halted for at least 14 days

A federal judge on July 20 temporarily paused the proposed merger between Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery just a week after 12 states sued to block the deal. U.S. District Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín granted a temporary restraining order in the case. She wrote in documents that the states raised “serious questions” on the merits of their claim, and that “the balance of equities and public interest tip sharply in favor” of the states. As a result, Paramount isn’t allowed to take any steps toward completing the merger with WBD. “This is a critical first win in our case to ensure this megamerger never sees the light of day,” California Attorney General Rob Bonta said in a statement to the Associated Press. “History tells the tale of what happens when a few people have great power over markets that are central to Americans’ lives: fewer opportunities for more people, worse products and services for all people.” The pause will last at least two weeks, and could be longer depending on how the legal process unfolds.

Keep up with all the latest in entertainment news

Subscribe to CableTV.com's email newsletter for the latest TV event viewing guides, entertaining takes, and money-saving promotions.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

Is the Paramount-WBD deal dead?

Not quite. The judge’s temporary restraining order is merely a sidestep in this particular legal process and gives the 12 states suing to block the transaction more time to present their case in court. The next major hearing in the case is scheduled for August 3 — exactly 14 days from when the judge made her ruling. Both parties could agree to a later hearing date, but that would also mean the merger would be paused longer. Paramount has significant financial incentive for the proceedings not to drag on. If the merger isn’t closed by September 30, the company would have to pay WBD shareholders nearly $7 million each day until the deal gets done, per court documents filed in a separate, since-dismissed lawsuit trying to end the proposed merger. Of note: The European Commission approved the merger on July 22 after conducting its own investigation.

Experts weigh in on the pause

Lawyers who spoke to CableTV.com say there isn’t any real danger for the merger to fail as a result of the restraining order. What really matters, they say, is that August 3 hearing. “That is where the states have to show a likelihood of success on the merits, and that is where this deal’s near-term fate gets decided,” Braden Perry, a litigation, regulatory, and government investigations attorney with Kennyhertz Perry, told me. The states have to convince Martínez-Olguín that the Department of Justice erred in approving the merger. Perry describes this as an “unusual position” for the states to be in. He also says the restraining order mainly puts time pressure on the deal, particularly with how much money Paramount would owe WBD shareholders if it goes on too long. “This is a delay risk, not yet an existential one,” Perry says. Martin Gasparian, owner of Maison Law Modesto, seems most intrigued by Martínez-Olguín’s comments about how the deal would affect theatrical distribution. “ present compelling evidence that the combined firm resulting from the transaction will possess substantial market share in the wide-release theatrical distribution market,” Martínez-Olguín wrote in the order. “On this combined firm market share alone, the Court is persuaded that it can presume the proposed merger is likely to violate antitrust laws.” Gasparian says the argument that the merger could make it more difficult for smaller companies to enter the market is “solid.” “The deal might hit a snag if the two companies fail to come up with a good argument to show they didn’t violate the antitrust laws,” Gasparian told me. “As it stands now, it’s a tall order for them because the 12 states have already shown the deal’s impact and how it trampled on the competition laws.”

Why are people trying to block the Paramount-WBD merger?

The 12 attorneys general argue that merging Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery would reduce competition in the TV, film, and streaming landscape, and would lead to higher prices and reduced quality for consumers. It would hurt the theater industry especially, they say. Essentially, because the Paramount-WBD merger would eliminate competition among themselves, it would create a ripple effect across an entire industry that consists of other large, multi-faceted media companies. The previous lawsuit, brought by five consumers, argued similarly. The Writers Guild of America also recently sued to block the merger, citing anti-competition concerns and reduced opportunities for writers. Paramount disagrees. It says that a major driving force of the merger is to create a streaming service that can compete with Netflix, Amazon Prime Video, and Disney+. “Neither Paramount+ nor HBO Max alone has the scale — in terms of subscriber base, revenue, or content library — to compete effectively with the leading platforms,” court documents state. Paramount argues that, post-merger, the combined company will put even more resources into theatrical releases, promising a 45-day window for films before they head to streaming.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts — we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • California Leads 12-State Effort to Block Paramount/Warner Bros. Deal
  • Fubo is Raising Prices By $15 — Here’s How It Justifies the Increase
  • Bundesliga Moves to USA Network and Fandango in New U.S. Rights Deal

item.title: Best Home Internet Services Across the US in 2026
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Home internet service is a necessity, but also a pain. That’s why our internet editor did the research for you and picked the Best of the Best of home internet in 2026 to get you the best providers, prices, and speeds.
item.content:encoded:
Best of the Best
Editorial rating (4.00/5)
Google Fiber
Price:
$70.00–$150.00/mo.
Download speed:
1,000–8,000 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
Check Availability
Read full review
Google Fiber
Price:
$70.00–$150.00/mo.
Download speed:
1,000–8,000 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
Editor's Choice
Editorial rating (3.70/5)
AT&T Fiber
Price:
$50.00–$125.00/mo.
Download speed:
300–5,000 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
View Plansfor AT&T
Read full review
AT&T Fiber
Price:
$50.00–$125.00/mo.
Download speed:
300–5,000 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
Best Bang for Your Buck
Editorial rating (3.90/5)
T-Mobile 5G Home Internet
Price:
$35.00–$80.00/mo.
Download speed:
up to 498 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
Check Availability
Read full review
T-Mobile 5G Home Internet
Price:
$35.00–$80.00/mo.
Download speed:
up to 498 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
Best of the Best for Cable
Editorial rating (3.80/5)
Xfinity
Price:
$45.00–$100.00/mo.
Download speed:
300–2,000 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited
View Plans for Xfinity
Read full review
Xfinity
Price:
$45.00–$100.00/mo.
Download speed:
300–2,000 Mbps
Data cap:
Unlimited

Data effective as of July 22, 2026. Compatible device and internet connection required. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change.

✦ At CableTV.com, we’ve been helping folks find the best home internet and TV services in their area since 2007. Get an in-depth look at how we evaluate internet services on our Why You Can Trust CableTV.com page.

Who is the best home internet provider?

Between family video calls, YouTube streaming, and web browsing, home internet is more essential than ever. But what home internet providers offer the best combination of price and performance? Our top ISPs differ heavily on the fine print, as some providers run cables through your neighborhood while others utilize wireless towers to deliver internet service to your home. But regardless of the technology, all five of our best ISPs provide internet service that’s fast, reliable, and a great value. We awarded Google Fiber our Best of the Best Award for the top internet service provider (ISP) overall this year. If you can get it at your address, we think you should snap it up. If you can’t get Google Fiber in your area, we also gave top marks to AT&T Fiber, Xfinity, and T-Mobile 5G Home Internet in this year’s guide. Read on to see our data-rich breakdown of the top ISPs in the U.S.

Jump to:
  • What to look for when choosing an internet service
  • Top home internet services at a glance
  • Best of the Best Award winners up close
  • Honorable mentions

Compare America’s best home internet services at a glance

Internet service providers comparison table

Internet provider Price Max download speed Data cap Details
Google Fiber
Best of the Best 		$70.00–$150.00/mo. 1,000–8,000 Mbps Unlimited
View Plansfor Google Fiber
AT&T Fiber
Editor's choice 		$50.00–$125.00/mo. 300–5,000 Mbps Unlimited
View Plansfor AT&T
T-Mobile 5G Home Internet
Best Bang for Your Buck 		$35.00–$80.00/mo.** up to 498 Mbps Unlimited
View Plansfor T-Mobile
Xfinity
Best for Cable 		$45.00–$100.00/mo. 300–2,000 Mbps Unlimited View Plans for Xfinity
Ziply Fiber
Best for Reliability 		$20.00–$900.00/mo. 50–50,000 Mbps Unlimited View Plans for Ziply
Verizon Fios
Best for Gaming 		$49.99–$109.99/mo. 300–2,300 Mbps Unlimited View plan
Cox $55.00–$115.00/mo. 300–2,000 Mbps 1.2 TB View plan
Frontier $29.99–$109.99/mo. 200–7,000 Mbps Unlimited
View Plansfor Frontier
Optimum $25.00–$90.00/mo. 300–8,000 Mbps Unlimited View plan
Spectrum $40.00–$70.00/mo. Up to 500–2,000 Mbps Unlimited View plan
Starlink $55.00–$175.00/mo. Up to 400 Mbps 50 GB–Unlimited View plan

Data effective as of July 22, 2026. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change. *Price per month with Auto Pay & without select 5G mobile plans. Consumer data usage is subject to the usage restrictions set forth in Verizon’s terms of service; visit: https://www.verizon.com/support/customer-agreement/ for more information about 5G Home Internet or https://www.verizon.com/about/terms-conditions/verizon-customer-agreement for Fios internet. **Plus taxes and fees.

Best of the Best Award internet winners up close

Every year, we highlight the nation’s top internet providers based on factors like pricing, performance, and CableTV.com’s annual customer satisfaction polling. Keep on reading to find out more about our top picks for the Best of the Best for home internet service.

Price: $70.00–$150.00/mo.

Max download speeds: 1,000–8,000 Mbps

Data cap: Unlimited

View Google Fiber Plans

Best of the Best: Google Fiber

Why we love it: Fast fiber internet, no annual price increases, few if any outages, and best Wi-Fi router/modem offers Why we hate it: Limited availability and no TV bundlesGoogle Fiber earned our Best of the Best Award because it dominates the competition across the board. To start with, Google Fiber landed in the top three overall on our annual customer satisfaction survey of over 8,000 real customers for the third year running. Plus, Google Fiber offers excellent speeds of up to 8 Gbps and stellar performance. An impressive 84% of CableTV.com survey respondents were extremely satisfied with Google Fiber’s speeds (tied for the highest ranking), and 81% of respondents were equally satisfied with Google Fiber’s service reliability. If you’re looking for high-performance fiber internet service, Google Fiber will deliver, and it won’t go down.

With Google Fiber, you’ll also get add-ons like a free modem/router, free Google Drive storage space, flat-rate pricing, and no annual price increases. As our internet price investigation showed, Google hasn’t raised the price on its 1 Gbps plan since 2021. Google Fiber might not have the best reach—at the moment, it’s available in around 40 U.S. cities—and it’s facing competition from expanding competitors like T-Mobile and Verizon. But when it’s available, it’s impressive. (Two of us on the editorial staff here at CableTV.com have Google Fiber; it performs way better than our previous ISPs.) In this year’s J.D. Power ISP Satisfaction Survey, Google Fiber also blew out competitors in the South region by a whopping 100-point margin.

What do real customers think about Google Fiber?

“Google Fiber has been consistent with my Internet service. They help me address any IT issues related to my Internet. The customer service representatives go out of their way to help address any questions, concerns or problems you may have with your Internet.”

Alaina S., Missouri
Fiber internet will always be faster and more reliable than the competition (read our fiber internet guide to find out why). With just a few straightforward plans, Google makes it easy to access high speeds immediately. And those are just some of the reasons why we love Google Fiber. To find out more and to see if Google Fiber is in your area, read our comprehensive review.

Price: $50.00–$125.00/mo.

Max download speeds: 300–5,000 Mbps

Data cap: Unlimited

View Plansfor AT&T

Editor’s Choice: AT&T Fiber

Why we love it: Reliably fast internet speeds and high customer satisfaction. Why we love it: Expensive plans and limited bundle options.AT&T’s decent-to-middling scores for its 5G and DSL services kept it out of the conversation for our best overall ISPs. But if your neighborhood’s fortunate enough to get AT&T Fiber, you’ll get one of the best internet plans on the market. We like that every AT&T Fiber plan avoids tricks like price increases after 12 months, restrictive contracts, or early termination fees. AT&T internet is contract-free, so you can get internet on your terms when and how you want it. AT&T Fiber was also no slouch when it came to performance in our customer satisfaction survey. AT&T Fiber ranked second in overall satisfaction among fiber ISPs with an 83% approval rating and broke the 80% approval mark for internet speed and reliability satisfaction. AT&T’s fiber availability remains a work in progress, it’s generally pricier than the competition, and AT&T’s lack of TV or streaming bundle deals is a bummer. That said, AT&T offers solid cell phone bundle savings, and it’s one of the biggest fiber ISPs nationwide. Dollars to donuts, we think that no-contract AT&T fiber internet is worth the price.

What do real customers think about AT&T?

“While there are cheaper options, I'm satisfied with the price because since I work from home, it's important to have fast internet speeds, and I feel like AT&T delivers on that. AT&T has probably been the best provider in my area for reliability. The one time we had an outage so far, it only lasted about an hour. I've had experiences with other providers in Miami where there were outages almost once a month and lasting several hours.”

Natassia C., Florida

Price: $35.00–$80.00/mo.

Max download speeds: up to 498 Mbps

Data cap: Unlimited

View Plans for T-Mobile

Bang for Your Buck/Best for 5G: T-Mobile 5G Home Internet

Why we love it: Bundle discounts up to $25/mo., no annual price increases, and wide availability Why we hate it: Slower download and upload speeds and less reliable serviceT-Mobile 5G Home Internet is a great deal for most people because it cuts the bloat and provides solid internet service at a great price. T-Mobile 5G Home Internet starts at $60 per month (plus taxes and fees) with its Rely plan, and if you bundle a T-Mobile phone plan, you can drop your bill down to only $35 monthly, which is miles ahead of competing cable and fiber ISPs. Best of all, T-Mobile’s flat-rate pricing means you’ll always pay the same price for internet service. You won’t have to deal with cable ISP tricks like second-year price increases or service contracts. Your dollar will also go further on T-Mobile than on Verizon. T-Mobile 5G Home Internet will give you a bundle discount and five-year price guarantee with any postpaid phone plan, while Verizon only offers a five-year price lock on their most expensive 5G home internet plan. Respondents in CableTV.com’s annual customer survey also agreed, as 87% of T-Mobile 5G Home Internet customers approved of their prices compared to competitors, versus only 68% of Verizon’s customers, which is why we also named it Best of the Best for 5G Home Internet.

We think T-Mobile 5G Home Internet speeds are fast enough for most home use. With speeds up to 498 Mbps, you’ll usually have enough bandwidth for Netflix nights, TikTok swiping, and everything in between. You’ll get spectacular bang for your buck if T-Mobile 5G Home Internet is available in your area. T-Mobile also outperformed Verizon 5G Home Internet in our performance and satisfaction polling. That said, because T-Mobile 5G Home Internet relies on a cell phone network, you may experience inconsistent download and upload speeds and more frequent outages than you would with a cable or fiber internet provider. The value is there, but it does come with some tradeoffs. Check out our comprehensive T-Mobile 5G Home Internet review to learn more about the service.

What do real customers think about T-Mobile 5G Home Internet?

“It’s good but where I live it's hard to find a strong signal. So sometimes, my stuff has to load up before it lets me watch anything and that can be annoying but the price was nicer than my old provider.”

Diana M., Tennessee

Price: $45.00–$100.00/mo.

Max download speeds: 300–2,000 Mbps

Data cap: Unlimited

View Plans for Xfinity

Best of the Best for Cable: Xfinity

Why we love it: Cheap internet plans, good internet speeds, and optional no-contract plans Why we hate it: Monthly hidden fees and middling customer satisfaction scoresThese days, we’d understand if getting cable internet feels like settling for third place, especially considering the speed of fiber internet or the flexibility of 5G home internet. But Xfinity did the work to earn its spot on this year’s top ISPs list. For starters, Xfinity won best-in-class honors among cable ISPs in our 2025 Internet Customer Satisfaction Survey:

  • 76% of surveyed Xfinity customers were fully or highly satisfied with Xfinity’s internet speeds.
  • 78% of respondents approved of Xfinity’s internet reliability.
  • 75% of respondents were fully or very satisfied with their Xfinity internet service.
Additionally, Xfinity Mobile ranked as a top ISP cell phone plan in our annual customer satisfaction survey. What’s more, Xfinity’s TV plans landed in our best cable TV provider rankings. Xfinity’s internet plan catalog covers every budget range, and you’ll be equally set if you’re looking to bundle TV or mobile phone service. Most significantly, Xfinity’s five-year price guarantee is a game-changer that answers many of our long-standing gripes with the ISP. While Xfinity isn’t without downsides such as average customer service and hidden fees, most customers will gladly take benefits like unlimited data, no price hikes, and free internet hardware. Fiber remains our top pick for internet, but if you have to settle for cable internet in your area, Xfinity’s made huge strides as an ISP. Read our full Xfinity internet review to see why it earned its spot as our favorite cable ISP.

What do real customers think about Xfinity?

“We have been a customer for 10 years and have virtually no problems with them. They are very responsive to any questions we may have.”

Ilene V., Pennsylvania

Price: $20.00–$900.00/mo.

Max download speeds: 50–50,000 Mbps

Data cap: Unlimited

View Plans for Ziply

Best of the Best for Reliability: Ziply Fiber

Why we love it: No annual contracts, stellar internet speeds, and great satisfaction scores Why we hate it: Extremely limited availability and aging DSL networkIf you have a need for speed and live in the right parts of the Pacific Northwest, you’ll want to spring for Ziply Fiber’s internet plans. On paper, Ziply offers an excellent range of fiber plans with standard features like no-contract service and budget-friendly basic tiers. But more importantly, Ziply Fiber customers vouch for the ISP’s network quality. Ziply Fiber led all providers in our reliability ratings thanks to strong marks for internet speed (86% approval) and reliability (83%). If that wasn’t enough, Ziply also earned strong marks for areas like installation ease (86%), overall satisfaction (87%), and customer support (83%).

What do real customers think about Ziply Fiber?

Best in our area. Coverage can be hard. I like the price per month. If outage occurs, seems to be fixed in reasonable amount of time.

Lori P., Washington
That said, Ziply’s availability is hamstrung by a network that’s small even by regional ISP standards. Ziply covers only chunks of Washington, Oregon, Idaho, and Montana—you’ll also need to be in a region that has fiber instead of DSL service. Still, Ziply offers some of the best fiber internet performance around, and we fully recommend it if you’re fortunate enough to be in a city with Ziply coverage. Check out our Ziply Fiber Internet review for a full breakdown of its specs, speeds, and more.

Price: $49.99–$109.99/mo.

Max download speeds: 300–2,300 Mbps

Data cap: Unlimited

View plan

Best of the Best for Gaming: Verizon Fios

Why we love it: Excellent latency times and fiber internet performance Why we hate it: Higher pricing versus the competitionVerizon has long been one of our top ISPs thanks to its excellent home internet offerings, and its strong fiber network helped it earn our spot for the best ISP for gamers. According to latency data from our partners at HighSpeedInternet.com, Verizon Fiber led all of our ISPs with an average mark of 10.7 milliseconds. For gamers, low latency is key to avoiding game losses due to frame rate drops or buffering. Similarly, you’ll generally get solid to excellent performance and great internet deals with Verizon Fios. The provider had overall and performance satisfaction ratings of more than 80% in CableTV.com’s internet customer satisfaction polling, while J.D. Power named Verizon as its top-ranked East region ISP in its annual Satisfaction Study. (However, Verizon’s pricing generally left customers wanting compared to competitors like AT&T and Google Fiber.) Keep in mind that Verizon Fios is different than Verizon 5G Home Internet. Verizon 5G Home Internet uses Verizon’s 5G data network to deliver home internet, so you’ll get lower rates but slower speeds than Verizon Fios. Visit our full Verizon Fios and Verizon 5G Home Internet reviews to learn more about Verizon’s home internet services.

What do real customers think about Verizon Fios?

I don't necessarily think their customer service department is the greatest, but the service itself has been so reliable and great, I have so few issues.

Adam B., New York

Honorable mentions: Other top internet service providers

Price: $55.00–$175.00/mo. Download speed: Up to 400 Mbps Data cap: 50 GB—Unlimited

Why we love it: Excellent satisfaction and great satellite internet download speeds Why we hate it: High pricing compared to cable or 5G internet

View plan

Starlink

Satellite internet’s typically been a last resort for rural or remote homes, but Starlink’s bucked the trend in several respects. For one, Starlink customers have consistently raved about the satellite ISP in CableTV.com’s customer satisfaction surveys. This year, Starlink ranked first overall in customer satisfaction and second overall for reliability—the ISP’s ability to outperform expectations for rural internet shoppers is why it’s increasingly made satellite internet a two-horse race. Of course, satellite internet’s not designed to be a cable or DSL internet alternative. While Starlink has done a lot to reduce its pricing and fees, you’ll generally pay more in service and upfront costs on Starlink than with a traditional ISP. But if your country home has had to settle for LTE internet speeds or dated satellite internet hardware, you’ll be in great hands with Starlink.

Price: $30.00–$175.00/mo. Download speed: 300–5,000 Mbps Data cap: 400 GB—Unlimited

Why we love it: Solid customer satisfaction ratings, full bundle slate Why we hate it: Staggered data caps

View plan

Astound Broadband

Thanks to fiber and 5G home internet, cable ISPs generally have to work harder to get customer attention these days. Astound doesn’t have the national profile of its larger competitors, but it’s still a solid-enough alternative if your area lacks fiber internet coverage. For starters, Astound averaged a respectable 75% approval rating for overall satisfaction and internet performance in our satisfaction polling—it beat Spectrum by about 5% across all three fields. Plus, Astound offers a deep bench of home phone, cell phone, and TV bundle options for internet customers interested in bundling services. That said, Astound’s use of data caps and overage charges is one of the provider’s bigger black marks. Most ISPs have abandoned hard data caps or moved to soft throttling due to negative customer feedback, but Astound has rising caps up to 1 TB on its sub-1 Gbps internet plans—you’ll get unlimited data only with a 1 Gbps or faster internet plan.

Pro tip: Click the “Change zip” button below to see providers in other areas.

What to look for in a home internet service

Some internet services have exciting deals and freebies that look super impressive. But when you want the best home internet provider, make sure the bang for your buck, reliability, features, and customer satisfaction ratings meet your standards before you sign a contract.

Bang for your buck

Since there’s no standard for internet pricing, even budget plans can run anywhere from $19.99 to $50.00 per month. (It’s a big range, we know.) Value depends a lot on your providers and location, but we used a few factors to determine which ISP offers the best internet value. Nearly all of our top internet service providers have no annual price increases. We wish that was the case for every ISP out there, but that’s not yet reality. Other ISPs will hike up the price after a year of service, making your once affordable plan much pricier than what you originally were paying. We also prioritize providers that are transparent with any extra fees that may be incurred—like, say, potential installation or router rental fees—along with providers that have a simple pricing method with all fees included.

Reliability

Our internet usage is growing all the time, and our bandwidth needs are higher than ever. With that in mind, you need internet that’s consistently fast and stable at your home. As part of our annual Internet Customer Satisfaction Survey series, we talked to thousands of real-life internet customers to see how they felt about their ISPs. Their feedback on their ISP’s speed and reliability (which refers to how often their internet goes down) played a huge role in our rankings. Two of our five top choices for best ISPs are fiber providers, which deliver more reliability than a cable, satellite, 5G, or DSL internet provider.

Features

Internet service providers are in the business of offering way more than internet these days. We gave the highest marks to ISPs that offered the best catalog of add-ons, which can include a number of options. Bundle choices were a huge plus in our eyes, as many offer services ranging from TV service to cheaper cell phone plans. We didn’t talk about data caps much in this article because most of our top recommendations offer unlimited data. We also favored ISPs that offered free equipment options. Most ISPs will give you the option to rent your equipment for a monthly fee or potentially buy it outright upfront. Even better, some providers, like Google Fiber and T-Mobile 5G Home Internet, won’t charge you at all. If equipment isn’t free, we suggest buying your wireless router instead of renting one since you’ll save on monthly rental fees.

Customer satisfaction

Customer service takes into account how efficiently ISPs get their customers the help they need. Some providers have their customer service desks staffed 24/7, while some process all requests through support pages and emails. Some ISPs are national chains with stores across the country to drop into, while others are largely online affairs. Regardless of how they solve customers’ problems, we prioritize how consistently and efficiently they do so, and our top picks all scored high marks on our survey.

Best home internet services FAQ

Why you should trust us

To find this year’s best internet providers, we spent thousands of hours analyzing each ISP’s plans based on factors including price, reliability, and features like a free modem/router. We also relied on our annual internet customer satisfaction survey to find out how current customers felt about their ISPs. Not only that, but we did hands-on testing of our own home internet to round out our ratings of select providers. For more information on our methodology, check out our How We Rank page. Like the way we roll? Return to our list of the best ISPs in your area.

Get online with CableTV.com

  • 2026 CableTV.com Internet Customer Satisfaction Survey
  • Best Fiber Internet Providers
  • Best 5G Home Internet Providers
  • Free, Cheap, and Low-Income Internet Service Guide

item.title: Back-to-School Savings: Xfinity Unveils Exclusive Student Deals with $200 Gift Cards and Free Peacock
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Xfinity unveils back-to-school internet deals for college students, featuring same-day setup, prepaid Mastercards, and free Peacock Premium for 2 years.
item.content:encoded:
Xfinity’s giving students $200 for a limited time

As summer is beginning to wind down, college students across the country are finalizing packing lists and prepping for their moves back to campus. Internet setup isn’t exactly high on the move-in day excitement list, but trust me—it’s a priority. Having zippy speeds and a reliable connection is essential for students who don’t want to spend late nights in the on-campus library finishing their assignments (been there, done that). That’s why Xfinity—our pick for the best cable internet provider currently on the market—is tailoring its current deals specifically to students who need dependable internet access for the coming school year. Here are the details: Students who sign up for 500 Mbps internet will get a $100 Mastercard from Xfinity, along with same-day setup. Need gig-level speeds? Built for households where all the roomies will be taking Zoom classes and streaming TV, Xfinity’s 1 Gig plan gives new student sign-ups a $200 prepaid Mastercard, plus Peacock Premium included for two years. Not too shabby.

Hook it up with the hottest in TV and internet

Stay tuned for the latest deals, show premieres, and more by signing up for our newsletter.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

How to get Xfinity’s back-to-school student deals

College expenses add up fast—between tuition, textbooks, rent, and dining plans, student budgets are stretched thin from day one. Getting a prepaid Mastercard can help offset some of the smaller move-in expenses, giving you some totally free cash for groceries or desk supplies (or whatever else floats your boat—no judgment here). The inclusion of same-day setup and a two-year Peacock Premium sub is the cherry on top. Students who want to take advantage of these limited-time promos can get all the details, including terms and conditions and student eligibility criteria, from our Xfinity deals guide.Don’t go to school in an Xfinity area? If you’ve checked your address and live outside of an Xfinity service area, not to fear. We have a guide dedicated to the best cheap internet packages to help save you some cash this school year.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts — we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news

  • Fubo is Raising Prices By $15—Here’s How It Justifies the Increase
  • No Refunds: PlayStation Takes Movies Away From Customers Who Purchased VOD
  • Apple TV is Giving Out Free 4K Upgrades to Its Customers

item.title: Where To Watch ‘Yellowjackets’: Streaming and Cable
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Wondering where to watch Yellowjackets? Our experts verified every way to stream all 3 seasons on Paramount+ or find the SHOWTIME cable channel!
item.content:encoded:
✦ Edited and fact-checked by Randy Harward, our Entertainment Editor with over 25 years covering TV and entertainment.”Yellowjackets” follows a girls’ high school soccer team who survive a plane crash in the wilderness, told across dual timelines as the adult survivors grapple with what they did to stay alive. The psychological horror hit streams on Paramount+ with the Premium plan and airs on the Paramount+ with SHOWTIME linear channel. We recommend Paramount+ Premium if you want to stream on your own schedule, since the show’s fourth and final season doesn’t have a confirmed premiere date yet.

Keep up with all the latest in entertainment news

Subscribe to CableTV.com's email newsletter for the latest TV viewing guides, entertaining takes, and money-saving promotions.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

Where to watch “Yellowjackets” on streaming and cable TV

Streaming service Cable channel
Paramount+ Paramount+ with SHOWTIME
View Plans See Channel Number in Your Area

Data effective as of post date. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change.

When does “Yellowjackets” stream on Paramount+?

Yes, all three existing seasons of “Yellowjackets” stream now on the Paramount+ Premium plan (formerly called Paramount+ with SHOWTIME). Season 4, confirmed as the show’s final season, began filming in February 2026, but Paramount hasn’t announced a premiere date as of this writing.

When does “Yellowjackets” air on the Paramount+ with SHOWTIME channel?

New episodes have historically debuted on the Paramount+ with SHOWTIME linear channel on Sundays, a day or two after their streaming premiere, and past seasons continue to rerun on the channel. We expect the same release pattern once Season 4 gets a release date.

What channel is Paramount+ with SHOWTIME?

City DIRECTV channel DISH channel Optimum channel Spectrum channel Xfinity channel
New York, NY 545/546 318/319 320/327 551 —
Los Angeles, CA 545/546 318/319 — 551 —
Chicago, IL 545/546 318/319 — — 185
Houston, TX 545/546 318/319 — — 474
Phoenix, AZ 545/546 318/319 — — —

Data effective as of post date. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change.

Don’t see your hometown or provider? We’ve got channel number guides for every popular local and cable TV channel, including Paramount+ with SHOWTIME, in over 50 U.S. cities.

Which live TV providers have “Yellowjackets”

Provider Price Live cable channel Streaming service bundle or add-on Details
Cox $20.00–$184.00/mo. ✔ ✔ View Plans for Cox

Read Cox review
Best for Sports

Read DIRECTV review

Read DISH review

Read Fubo review

Read Hulu + Live TV review

Read Optimum review

Find the best TV provider near you

Want a TV plan that keeps you covered beyond episodes of “Yellowjackets”? Enter your zip code below to see your area’s top-ranked TV and internet bundles.

Where to watch “Yellowjackets” FAQ

CableTV.com editors created this article using original analysis and proprietary inputs, with AI tools assisting in drafting. The final content was reviewed and edited for accuracy and quality.

item.title: Sophie Thatcher’s 8 Best Performances: ‘Yellowjackets,’ Star Wars, and More
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: From surviving the wilderness in Yellowjackets to facing A24 horror in Heretic, we rank Sophie Thatcher’s best roles in film and TV.
item.content:encoded:
Sophie Thatcher has quickly become one of the most exciting young actors to watch, effortlessly jumping between gritty indie sci-fi, major franchises, and elevated horror. While you might know her best as the rebellious teen Natalie in “Yellowjackets,” her filmography is stacked with cool, edgy, and intense roles that prove she’s a scream queen in the making. Today, I’m ranking her best projects to date. In the TikTok post below, we highlighted a few favorites, but I’ve expanded the list to include eight essential performances that showcase her range — from hunting bounties in a galaxy far, far away to surviving Stephen King nightmares. Check out our original list on TikTok!

1. “Yellowjackets” (2021)

This is the role that launched Thatcher into the stratosphere. Playing the teenage version of Natalie “Nat” Scatorccio, she perfectly channels the grit, trauma, and rebellious spirit that Juliette Lewis brings to the adult version. Stranded in the wilderness after a plane crash, Nat is the group’s hunter and arguably its moral compass (even if she holds a gun while doing it). It’s a raw, physical performance that anchors the show’s retro 1996 timeline.

Watch on Paramount+

2. “Prospect” (2018)

Before she was a Yellowjacket, Thatcher starred alongside Pedro Pascal in this atmospheric indie sci-fi gem. She plays Cee, a teenage girl traveling with her father to a poisonous moon to harvest gems. When things go wrong (as they always do in space westerns), she has to team up with Pascal’s rogue character to survive. It’s a quiet, tense film where Thatcher proves she can carry a movie almost entirely on her own.

3. “The Book of Boba Fett” (2021)

Joining the Star Wars universe is a bucket-list item for any actor, and Thatcher made her mark as Drash, the leader of a street gang of cyborgs (the Mods) on Tatooine. With her cybernetic arm and brightly colored Vespa-style speeder, she brought a cyber-punk aesthetic to the dust of Mos Espa. She’s tough, loyal, and honestly one of the coolest parts of Boba Fett’s new crew.

Watch on Disney+

4. “Heretic” (2024)

Thatcher continues her horror streak in this A24 thriller, starring alongside Hugh Grant and Chloe East. As Sister Barnes, she plays one of two Mormon missionaries who knock on the wrong door. When they become trapped in the home of the eccentric Mr. Reed (Grant), a battle of wits and faith ensues. Thatcher’s ability to portray creeping dread and innocent cleverness makes this a nail-biter you won’t want to miss.

Watch on HBO Max

5. “The Boogeyman” (2023)

Adapting a Stephen King short story is no small feat, but Thatcher grounds the supernatural scares with genuine emotion. She plays Sadie Harper, a grieving high school student dealing with the death of her mother, while a literal monster feeds on her family’s suffering. It’s a classic creature feature, and Thatcher sells every terrified scream and courageous stand against the darkness.

6. “Companion” (2025)

In this sci-fi thriller produced by the team behind “Barbarian,” Thatcher plays Iris. We won’t spill too much tea about the plot to preserve the twists, but the film is set to be a mind-bending ride involving artificial intelligence and twisted relationships. Given Thatcher’s track record with strange sci-fi, she fits right into this stylized, high-concept world.

Watch on HBO Max

7. “MaXXXine” (2024)

Thatcher joined the Ti West X trilogy in its final chapter, “MaXXXine.” Set in 1980s Hollywood, she plays an FX artist who crosses paths with Mia Goth’s titular starlet. Though it’s a supporting role, her punk-rock aesthetic and attitude fit perfectly into the film’s neon-soaked slasher vibe. It’s yet another credential on her growing horror resume.

8. “When the Streetlights Go On” (2020)

Originally released on Quibi (and now available on Roku), this crime drama is set in 1995 and follows the aftermath of a double homicide in a suburban town. Thatcher plays Becky Monroe, the sister of one of the victims. It’s a moody, nostalgic mystery that allows Thatcher to play the cool-girl archetype with a layer of tragic depth.

Best Sophie Thatcher performances FAQ

Who does Sophie Thatcher play in Star Wars?

Sophie Thatcher plays Drash in "The Book of Boba Fett." Drash is the leader of a group of cyborg street youths (the Mods) who work for Boba Fett on Tatooine.

Is Sophie Thatcher related to Margaret Thatcher?

No, Sophie Thatcher is an American actress and is not related to the former British Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher.

What is Sophie Thatcher best known for?

Sophie Thatcher is best known for her role as the teenage Natalie "Nat" Scatorccio in the Showtime series "Yellowjackets" and for her role in "The Book of Boba Fett."

Related articles

  • How To Watch “Yellowjackets”
  • The Best Scary Shows to Stream Right Now
  • What To Watch on Paramount+

item.title: We Need a Bigger Boat! Where To Watch Shark Week 2026
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Watch Shark Week 2026 on Discovery Channel, Max, YouTube TV, Hulu + Live TV and more. Stream all 20 new premieres July 26–Aug. 1.
item.content:encoded:
✦ Edited and fact-checked by Randy Harward, our Entertainment Editor with 25+ years covering horror and streaming.Shark Week 2026 is back for its 38th annual celebration of all things sharks. This year, Discovery Channel is premiering 20 all-new specials starting Sunday, July 26 at 8 p.m. ET/PT and running through Saturday, Aug. 1. The lineup includes fan-favorite franchises like “Air Jaws” plus bold new stories, prehistoric shark reconstructions using cutting-edge CGI, and unexpected collaborations that blend science with spectacle. Whether you are a longtime Shark Week devotee or a first-time viewer, you have got plenty of ways to tune in — from traditional cable to streaming services to free trials.

Looking for the hottest entertainment?

Subscribe to CableTV.com's email newsletter for the latest TV event viewing guides, entertaining takes, and money-saving promotions.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

Where to watch Shark Week 2026 on streaming and cable TV

Streaming service Cable channel
Max (formerly HBO Max) Discovery Channel
View Plans See Channel Number in Your Area
Data effective as of post date. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change.

When does Shark Week 2026 stream on HBO Max?

New episodes premiere live on Discovery Channel and stream the same night on HBO Max. Subscribers with access to live Discovery Channel can watch each nightly premiere at 8 p.m. ET/PT. If your HBO Max plan does not include live channels, episodes are typically available on demand the next day.

When does Shark Week 2026 air on Discovery Channel?

All 20 new specials air live on Discovery Channel nightly from July 26 through Aug. 1, with each episode premiering at 8 p.m. ET/PT. If you miss a live broadcast, you can catch reruns throughout the week. Cable subscribers can also stream episodes through the Discovery Go app with a valid cable login.

What channel is Discovery Channel?

City DIRECTV channel DISH channel Optimum channel Spectrum channel Xfinity channel

Los Angeles, CA 278 182 — 73 —
Chicago, IL 278 182 — — 51
Houston, TX 278 182 — 26 29
Phoenix, AZ 278 182 — — 43

Read Xfinity review

Read YouTube TV review

Shark Week remains one of the most accessible summer programming events, with Discovery Channel available on nearly every major cable, satellite, and live TV streaming bundle. Whether you are looking for the ultimate shark-themed experience or just want to catch the premiere, you have got options that fit your viewing style.

item.title: Fubo is Raising Prices By $15—Here’s How It Justifies the Increase
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Fubo just signed a new deal with NBC, and that means your bill is going up. Read on to learn why Fubo is raising prices.
item.content:encoded:
Good news and bad news for Fubo customers

I put the bad news in the headline, so let’s rip the bandage off. The live TV streaming service Fubo is getting more expensive to the tune of $15/mo. this month. Your total bill will vary depending on your plan, and whether you subscribe to regional sports networks, but either way you’re about to pay more money than you’d probably like. The good news is that you’re getting more content for the price. Fubo is bringing NBC channels back to its lineup, including all the sports that NBC hosts. That means you’ll get Sunday Night Football, Sunday Night Baseball, WNBA games, and more, all bundled into your Fubo subscription. Fubo had previously removed NBC channels from its lineup after disputes with NBCUniversal, but that dispute finally ended in June. Fubo is still missing Warner Bros. Discovery channels like AMC, BBC America, and the Food Network, as well as Versant channels like SYFY, USA Network, and MS NOW. Instead, Fubo mainly focuses on sports content and little else. Fubo’s got a lot of plans, and sports add-ons tend to get pretty confusing under the best of circumstances, so let’s dive into the details and figure out what this means for you and your TV.

Keep up with all the latest in entertainment news

Subscribe to CableTV.com's email newsletter for the latest TV event viewing guides, entertaining takes, and money-saving promotions.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

Which Fubo plans are changing?

Plan Old price New price
Fubo Sports (Sports + News) $55.99/mo. $64.99/mo.
Essential $73.99/mo. $88.99/mo.
Pro $73.99/mo. $88.99/mo.
Elite $83.99/mo. $98.99/mo.
Ultimate $98.99/mo. $113.99/mo.
Deluxe $103.99/mo. $118.99/mo.
All of Fubo’s regular plans (Essential, Pro, Elite, Ultimate, and Deluxe) are going up by $15 per month. Its Core and Ultra plans, only available in select markets, are also going up by $15 per month. If you subscribe to the Fubo Sports service, also known as Sports + News, your plan will be going up by $9 per month. In addition, according to Fubo’s website, if you subscribe to a plan with a Regional Sports Fee and live in Boston, Philadelphia, or an area serviced by NBC Sports Bay Area or NBC Sports California, your fee will rise to $15.99/mo. All of these changes will go into effect when NBCUniversal channels become available in your region. Fubo first announced these changes back in June, but some are only going into effect now in July, which is why it may be on your radar. Unfortunately, there is no one single price-changing day to report: it will vary from person to person, so you should plan to see the change on your next bill. You can’t opt out of receiving NBCUniversal content on your existing plan, but you can downgrade your subscription if you’d like to keep your monthly bill low. You can also consider switching to another provider—we’ve got a lot of recommendations if you’re looking for a new live TV streaming service.

What does this mean for Latino subscribers?

There’s also good news and bad news for Latino subscribers. The good news is that Fubo Latino gained Telemundo and Universo back in June, so you’re already getting FIFA World Cup courage and lots of other Spanish-language sports. In fact, you got FIFA coverage before Fubo’s English-language customers. The bad news is that your plan also got more expensive for your first billing date on or after July 1. From now on, you’ll be paying $19.99/mo., rather than the $14.99/mo. it cost previously.

What channels is Fubo gaining with the price increase?

Thanks to this new deal, Fubo is gaining a bunch of NBCUniversal sports coverage. Unfortunately, this deal doesn’t include Versant channels, so Fubo is still missing favorites like SYFY, USA Network, and MS NOW. Your new lineup will change based on your plan and location, but here’s the full list of possible channels and networks:

  • NBC
  • Telemundo*
  • Bravo
  • Universo*
  • NBC Sports Regional Sports Channels
  • NBCSN
  • Cozi
  • NBC News NOW
  • True CRMZ
*also included with Fubo Latino
Of course, if it’s only NBC you’re after, you could always pick it up with an over-the-air (OTA) antenna. And if you’re looking for both live NBC coverage and Bravo content, Peacock Premium Plus has all you could want and more. But if you want a sports-focused streaming service and don’t mind shelling out the extra money, it is nice that Fubo has all of this extra coverage now. NBC hosts a lot of exclusive sports programming, like Sunday Night Football, Sunday Night Baseball, English Premier League, WNBA games, and more.

Fubo

Fubo has lost a lot of popular channels in recent years, but now it’s getting NBCUniversal programming back, adding to its already robust sports lineup.

YouTube TV

YouTube TV is our Editor’s Choice when it comes to sports-centric streaming services. Its robust channel lineup and reliable performance really wowed our Sports Editor.

Peacock

Peacock is a great alternative if you’re only looking for NBCUniversal content. It’s got titles from NBC, Bravo, and more.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts — we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • 12 States Try to Block Paramount Deal
  • World Cup Viewership Hits Record Highs
  • How Much Does a Movie Ticket Cost in 2026?

item.title: Bundesliga Moves to USA Network and Fandango in New U.S. Rights Deal
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Germany’s top soccer league reached a broadcast agreement with USA Network and Fandango. Here’s what the deal means for Bundesliga fans in the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
USA Network and Fandango will carry all Bundesliga matches

Germany’s top-flight soccer league has a new broadcast home in the U.S. USA Sports acquired exclusive English-language rights to air Bundesliga games through the 2030-2031 season, the company announced. The league’s 300-plus live matches will air on USA Network and stream on Fandango (formerly Fandango at Home) starting in August. USA Network will carry at least 30 matches featuring the top Bundesliga teams and rivalries. Every other match will stream for free on Fandango. “The Bundesliga boasts some of the most well-known clubs and players in the world and has an established connection with American soccer fans,” Matt Hong, president of USA Sports, said in a statement. “Our partnership with the Bundesliga represents another significant milestone in the growth of USA Sports as we continue to build a premier destination for live sports and deliver more of the events fans care about most.”

What the new Bundesliga TV deal means for U.S. fans

Before this deal, fans mainly saw Bundesliga games through ESPN+, with select games airing on ESPN, ESPN2, and even ABC. For the few games on ABC, at least, German football lovers could use a digital antenna to watch them for free. But there’s really no way to access USA Network for free — not for the long term, anyway. Now, fans will absolutely need a paid TV plan for more than two dozen games. The good news: Fandango will stream a ton of games for free. For Spanish-language fans — or people who just prefer watching football in Spanish — Telemundo signed a three-year deal with the Bundesliga to broadcast games. Telemundo and Universo will air more than 100 games over the air and on cable, and every game will stream live on Peacock. Fans of Premier League soccer are already accustomed to watching games on USA Network and Telemundo. But Bundesliga fans new to the setup will need a TV plan to watch matches on USA Network, Telemundo, and Universo once the season starts.

Bundesliga looks to build on World Cup viewing momentum

Record numbers of viewers have been tuning in to the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Historically, the global tournament has attracted new fans from every age demographic. It’s likely that trend will continue, and the Bundesliga is clearly banking on that with these new partnerships. “The 2026 World Cup has highlighted the tremendous growth potential of soccer in the U.S.,” said Robin Austermann, Bundesliga Americas Executive Vice President, in a statement. “We’ve seen that momentum firsthand, with the number of Bundesliga fans in the U.S. growing by 43% over the past five years. “Our partnership with Versant’s USA Sports marks the next phase of our strategy, combining outstanding distribution with significant growth opportunities by bringing the Bundesliga closer to more fans across the United States than ever before.”

Keep up with all the latest in entertainment news

Subscribe to CableTV.com's email newsletter for the latest TV event viewing guides, entertaining takes, and money-saving promotions.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

CableTV.com verdict: A big win for U.S. fans, even if they have to pay more

If you’re thinking, “Where do I get USA Network?” after this deal, you’re likely not alone. USA Network hasn’t been part of NBCUniversal since January. That’s when Versant became an independent company after Comcast spun it off, taking USA Network, Fandango, Golf Channel, and many other NBCU brands with it. That corporate shuffle may make the Bundesliga’s new English-language setup a little harder to follow for fans who still associate USA Network with NBCUniversal. Fortunately, fans can look to DIRECTV, YouTube TV, Hulu + Live TV, or Sling TV to get USA Network. But, like I mentioned earlier, the days of occasional free marquee Bundesliga games on ABC are over. Be that as it may, more access to a league or sport fans love is never a bad thing. The above TV providers each have some sort of combination of USA Network, Telemundo, and Universo. And with the addition of Fandango, fans can still enjoy plenty of free soccer. In other words, if you’re in the U.S. and love German soccer (or are more curious after the World Cup), you have so many ways to watch live once the season kicks off on Friday, August 28. That just wasn’t the case before.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts — we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • World Cup Viewership Hits Record Highs: Will Casual Soccer Viewing Continue?
  • Jalen Brunson, Messi, Shohei Ohtani Highlight 2026 ESPYs Nominations
  • Premier League Fixture Release Brings Key Changes

item.title: Catty, Cringey, Lusty! 8 New Reality Series Streaming in July
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Our reality TV expert breaks down 7 new reality series coming to streaming in April 2026. Check out their curated picks.
item.content:encoded:
With “Love Island,” “Summer House,” and “Temptation Island” in the rearview for the beginning of the summer, there are still so many reality shows we can indulge in. Things are heating up in this summer forecast of mess. Here’s my suggestion: maybe instead of the sun burning our skin, why don’t we just stay home and watch reality TV unfold in front of us? Come on, there’s cool air and ice aplenty in there. To convince you further, there are plenty of shows returning to our TV screens, including The OGs of the OC – “The Real Housewives of Orange County”, the Korean makeover marvel “Better Late Than Single”, and a new show featuring Bravo’s Vanderpump Rules alum– “House of Stassi.” Put that sunscreen back in the drawer and bring on the mess! Here’s everything you need to know when it comes to July’s reality TV watchlist!

8. “Better Late Than Single” (July 7 – Netflix)

This Korean reality makeover show helps “eternal singles” find love by coaching them on communication, style, and more. They’re then put together in a house to see whether love blossoms with the other “eternal singles” or whether it leads to heartbreak. This sleeper hit will make you kick your feet, giggle, or yell at the television because you just want them to work!

Where to watch “Better Late Than Single”

You can catch the first and second season of “Better Late Than Single” out now on Netflix with a subscription.

7. “Big Brother 28” (July 9 – CBS, Paramount+ Premium)

”Big Brother” is back, and if you’re looking to dial into season 28, now is the time! “Big Brother” features houseguests who are cut off from the outside world and filmed 24/7. Within the house, they compete in challenges and form relationships, friendships, enemies, and alliances among the houseguests. If you’re looking for an Orwellian-esque MTV’s “The Real World,” jump into “Big Brother” as soon as possible!

Where to watch “Big Brother 28”

”Big Brother” airs Wednesday, Thursday, and Sunday at 8 p.m. ET on CBS, which you can watch on YouTube TV or Paramount+ Premium.

6. “The Real Housewives of Orange County” (July 8 – Bravo, Peacock)

”The Real Housewives of Orange County” is in its 20th season and has become the longest-running in the franchise. We see some old faces returning, including Vicki Gunvalson (aka the OG of the OC), and welcome newcomer Camella Garcia, former Playboy model and wrestler turned real estate agent.

Where to watch “The Real Housewives of Orange County”

You can catch “The Real Housewives of Orange County” weekly on Thursdays at 8 p.m. ET on Bravo, which you can get with YouTube TV. Or watch the next day on Peacock with a subscription.

5. “Project Runway” (July 9 – Freeform, Hulu, and Disney+)

Fresh off the catwalk, season 22 is already hitting the ground strutting. (Did you like that wordplay? Don’t roll your eyes!) Heidi Klum returns with Christian Siriano and a panel of A-list judges to launch one designer into superstardom. What’s at stake? Oh, just a cash prize of $200,000, a mentorship to help debut their brand, and a spread in “Elle” magazine. No big deal, right?

Where to watch “Project Runway”

Catch “Project Runway” on Thursdays at 10 p.m., but catch episodes on Hulu and Disney+ the next day.

4. “Married at First Sight” (July 12 – Peacock)

Have you ever thought of marrying someone sight unseen? No? Well, I have a show for you! “Married at First Sight” brings singles together with the help of relationship experts and walks them down the aisle. When the couples say “I do,” that’s where the real tests begin. Will the experts actually know what they’re doing in matching these couples? Will they stay in these marriages, or will they fall apart?

Where to watch “Married at First Sight”

Watch “Married at First Sight” every Thursday on Peacock with a subscription.

3. “Love Is Blind: UK – After the Alter” (July 12 – Netflix)

Of the “Love Is Blind” spinoffs, “Love Is Blind: UK” is top-tier entertainment. “Love Is Blind: UK” brings singles to find their match sight unseen. When they meet, they test their connection by mixing their worlds, including living together. Will they be ready to walk down the aisle and say, “I do”? “Love is Blind: UK – After the Altar” brings back our favorites (and not-so-favorites) as they talk about how life has been since their pod experiences and confront each other on old grudges.

Where to watch “Love Is Blind: UK – After the Alter”

Watch the three-episode “Love is Blind: UK – After the Alter” now on Netflix with a subscription.

3. “The Ultimatum: Marry or Move On” (July 15 – Netflix)

Known for hosting “Love Is Blind” and “Perfect Match,” Nick and Vanessa Lachey have graced us once again with one of Netflix’s most diabolical creations. “The Ultimatum: Marry or Move On” features couples who are at a crossroads. One wants to be married, and the other one is on the fence. They test their couple by switching partners with other couples to see if they are the right fit or if there’s someone else out there who may be a better match.

Where to watch “The Ultimatum: Marry or Move On”

Catch “The Ultimatum: Marry or Move On” on July 14, and the finale and reunion on July 22, on Netflix with a subscription.

1. “House of Stassi” (July 29 – Freeform, Hulu)

Love her or hate her, Stassi Schroeder will always be a mainstay of reality TV. Debuting on “Vanderpump Rules,” Stassi has always given us quick quips, running lines, and mountains of controversy. With “House of Stassi,” the audience will get to see why Stassi has continued to be a talked-about figure. Stassi is joined by her husband, Beau Clark, best friend Taylor Strecker, alums from “Vanderpump Rules,” Katie Maloney and Kristina Kelly, and more.

Where to watch “House of Stassi”

Tune into the season premiere of “House of Stassi” Wednesday, July 29 on Freeform, which is available with YouTube TV. Or watch it the next day on Hulu with a subscription.

Disney+

You can watch “Project Runway” with a Disney+ subscription ($11.99–$18.99/mo.).

Hulu

Catch “Project Runway” and “House of Stassi” with a Hulu subscription ($11.99–$18.99/mo.).

Netflix

Best for KidsYou’ll need a Netflix subscription ($8.99–$26.99/mo.) ($8.99–$26.99/mo.) to watch “Better Late Than Single,” “Love Is Blind: UK – After the Alter,” and “The Ultimatum: Marry or Move On.”

Paramount+

You can get your live CBS channel, and watch “Big Brother Season 28,” with a subscription to the Paramount+ Premium plan ($8.99–$13.99/mo.).

Peacock

A Peacock subscription ($7.99–$16.99/mo.) is one way to watch “The Real Housewives of Orange County” and “Married at First Sight.”

YouTube TV

Best overallYou can find live TV channels like Bravo, CBS and Freeform on this top-shelf live TV streaming service (($82.99/mo.).

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

What to binge next

  • What To Watch on Netflix
  • New Shows Coming to Netflix
  • What To Watch on Peacock

item.title: Where To Watch ‘Dexter’ on Streaming and Cable
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Find out where to watch Dexter, including Paramount+ with SHOWTIME streaming and cable channel numbers by city.
item.content:encoded:
✦ Edited and fact-checked by Mike Strayer, our Managing Editor with 11 years covering streaming and entertainment.
Status: Data verified on (7/15/26)
Dexter Morgan’s double life as a Miami blood-spatter analyst by day and vigilante serial killer by night made him one of TV’s most iconic antiheroes, and his storyline has recently been resurrected from the IP catecombs. Between its original eight seasons, “Dexter: New Blood,” “Dexter: Original Sin,” and “Dexter: Resurrection,” there’s a lot of Dark Passenger to track down. Good news: The entire Dexter universe is all available in one place.

Don't miss an update

Stay tuned for the latest deals, contests, and more.

By signing up, you agree to our Privacy Policy & Terms and Conditions.

Where to watch “Dexter” on streaming and cable TV

Swipe Left to See All →
Streaming serviceCable channel
Paramount+Paramount+ with SHOWTIME
View Plans for Paramount PlusSee Channel Number in Your Area

Data effective as of post date. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change.

When does “Dexter” stream on Paramount+?

Every episode of the original “Dexter” series is currently streaming on Paramount+, and it’s included in every Paramount+ plan tier. The newer spinoffs, “Dexter: Original Sin” and “Dexter: Resurrection,” require the Paramount+ Premium plan and air new seasons weekly.

When does “Dexter” air live on the Paramount+ with SHOWTIME channel?

Reruns of “Dexter” still air periodically on the Paramount+ with SHOWTIME linear channel (formerly SHOWTIME), so cable and satellite subscribers with that premium tier can catch episodes without opening an app. If you’re looking for premium TV recs, check out our full guide to what to watch on Paramount+.

What channel is Paramount+ with SHOWTIME?

City DIRECTV channel DISH channel Optimum channel Xfinity channel
New York, NY 545/546 318/319 320/327 —
Los Angeles, CA 545/546 318/319 — —
Chicago, IL 545/546 318/319 — 185
Houston, TX 545/546 318/319 — 474
Phoenix, AZ 545/546 318/319 — —

Data effective as of post date. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change.

Don’t see your hometown or provider? We’ve got channel number guides for every popular local and cable TV channel, including Paramount+ with SHOWTIME, in over 50 U.S. cities.

Which live TV providers have “Dexter”?

Provider Price Live cable channel Streaming service Details
Cox $20.00–$184.00/mo. ✔ (Incl. w/ Contour TV Ultimate) ✔ (Incl. w/ Contour TV Ultimate) View Plans for Cox

Read Cox review
Best for Sports
DIRECTV 		$84.99–$171.98/mo. ✔ (Incl. w/ Premier plan) ✔ (Incl. w/ Premier plan) View Plans for DIRECTV STREAM

Read DIRECTV review
DISH $89.99–$119.99/mo. — ✔ (Add-on) View Plans for DISH

Read DISH review
Fubo $14.99–$103.99/mo. — ✔ (Add-on) View Plans for FuboTV

Read Fubo review
Hulu + Live TV $88.99–$99.99/mo. — ✔ (Add-on) View Plans for Hulu

Read Hulu + Live TV review
Optimum $15.00–$140.00/mo. ✔ (Add-on) ✔ (Add-on) View Plans for Optimum

Read Optimum review
Sling TV $19.99–$60.99/mo. — ✔ (Add-on) View Plans for Sling TV

Read Sling TV review
Editor"s Choice
Spectrum 		$40.00–$145.00/mo. — ✔ (Add-on) Check Availability

Read Spectrum review
Best of the Best
Verizon Fios 		$105.00–$149.00/mo. — ✔ (Add-on) View Plans for Verizon

Read Verizon Fios review
Xfinity $65.00–$135.00/mo. — ✔ (Add-on) View Plans for Xfinity

Read Xfinity review
Best for Live TV Streaming
YouTube TV 		$82.99/mo. — ✔ (Add-on) View Plans for YouTube TV

Read YouTube TV review

Data effective as of post date. Offers and availability may vary by location and are subject to change.

Our editorial team weighs pricing, channel lineup, and customer satisfaction data to rank providers for our Best of the Best Awards. We recommend a DIRECTV streaming plan if you want live Paramount+ with SHOWTIME, want access to the Paramount+ app, and love live sports. If you’d rather stream on demand than deal with cable, Paramount+ with SHOWTIME also carries solid channel hubs, like CBS and BET, on top of every OG “Dexter” season.

Find the best TV provider near you

Want a TV plan that keeps you covered beyond episodes of “Dexter”? Enter your zip code below to see your area’s top-ranked TV and internet bundles.

Where to watch “Dexter” FAQ

CableTV.com editors created this article using original analysis and proprietary inputs, with AI tools assisting in drafting. The final content was reviewed and edited for accuracy and quality.
